lede: A look at April 28 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Anthony Volpe and Roman Polák, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: CBS News
events.desc: CBS News releases evidence of the Abu Ghraib torture and prisoner abuse. The photographs show rape and abuse from the American troops over Iraqi detainees.

events.title: Stern (magazine)
events.desc: The West German news magazine Stern begins publishing excerpts from the purported diaries of Adolf Hitler, later revealed to be forgeries.

events.title: The Dark Side of the Moon
events.desc: The Dark Side of the Moon by Pink Floyd, recorded in Abbey Road Studios goes to number one on the US Billboard chart, beginning a record-breaking 741-week chart run.

events.title: Treaty of Taipei
events.desc: The Sino-Japanese Peace Treaty (Treaty of Taipei) is signed in Taipei, Taiwan between Japan and the Republic of China to officially end the Second Sino-Japanese War.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: Nine German E-boats attack US and UK units during Exercise Tiger, the rehearsal for the Normandy landings, killing 946.

events.title: South Africa
events.desc: South African medical researcher Max Theiler develops the yellow fever vaccine at the Rockefeller Foundation in New York City.

events.title: Louis Paulhan
events.desc: Frenchman Louis Paulhan wins the 1910 London to Manchester air race, the first long-distance aeroplane race in the United Kingdom.

events.title: Prussian Secret Police
events.desc: A week after being arrested by the Prussian Secret Police, French police inspector Guillaume Schnaebelé is released on order of Wilhelm I, German Emperor, defusing a possible war.

events.title: University of Santo Tomas
events.desc: Establishment of the Pontifical and Royal University of Santo Tomas, the Catholic University of the Philippines and the largest Catholic university in the world.

events.title: Nichiren
events.desc: Nichiren, a Japanese Buddhist monk, propounds Namu Myōhō Renge Kyō for the first time and declares it to be the essence of Buddhism, in effect founding Nichiren Buddhism.

deaths.text: Carl Friedrich von Weizsäcker, German physicist and philosopher
Benito Mussolini, Italian journalist, fascist dictator and politician, 27th Prime Minister of Italy

observances.text: Christian feast day: Kirill of Turov (Orthodox, added to Roman Martyrology in 1969)
Christian feast day: Blessed Marie Louise Trichet
Christian feast day: Vitalis and Valeria of Milan
